method_atom: Reformulate a linear partial differential evolution equation governing a field driven by a prescribed advecting flow into an equivalent boundary-plus-volume integral equation system, so the computational support shrinks to the active medium and its interface rather than all of space. Solve the resulting operator as an eigenvalue problem to locate the critical control-parameter threshold at which a self-sustaining instability first grows, and use a forced (inhomogeneous) variant of the same integral operator to probe how close a given configuration sits to that onset threshold.
object_atom: Magnetic-field generation by an electrically conducting fluid (liquid sodium) moving in a finite cylindrical vessel — kinematic dynamos driven by Beltrami-like flows, external surrounding layers, and applied fields, as realized in the von Karman sodium (VKS) and Riga dynamo experiments.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the volume-to-integral reformulation of a flow-driven linear operator and its eigenvalue/threshold and forced-response analysis; the domain-specific part is the conducting-fluid magnetic dynamo in cylindrical laboratory geometry.
